title: Container With Most Water
problem_statement: Given heights, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container holding the most water.

examples.explanation: The maximum area is formed by lines at positions 1 and 8.

intuition: Use two pointers from both ends and move the pointer at the shorter line inward to try to increase area.

approaches.name: Two-pointer greedy
approaches.description: Start with the widest container and shrink it, always moving the shorter side to possibly find a taller line.
approaches.why_not: Brute force checking all pairs is O(n^2) and too slow.
interview_commentary: Always move the shorter boundary because the area is limited by the smaller height.
The widest possible width is considered first, and then the window shrinks to seek taller boundaries.
This greedy two-pointer strategy guarantees the maximum area in one pass.

interview_qa.a: Because the area is limited by the shorter side and moving it may increase the height.

interview_qa.q: Is it safe to skip moving the taller pointer?
interview_qa.a: Yes, moving the taller one cannot improve area if the shorter side remains the same.
